_meta.method: Cross-referenced Sōtōshū outside-Japan directory (sotozen.com), Schweizerische Buddhistische Union (sbu.net), AZI (zen-azi.org), Kosen Sangha (zen-deshimaru.com), bouddha.ch national directory, Hanazono IRIZ archive, Kwan Um Europe, and individual sangha websites. Each entry has at minimum a primary URL and a corroborating source URL.
_meta.coverageEstimate: ~80% of publicly listed Swiss Zen sanghas across SōtōShū, AZI/Deshimaru, Kosen, Sanbō Zen, Plum Village, Kwan Um, and Kobun-lineage groups. Smaller Plum Village lay sanghas (which proliferate informally) are under-represented.
knownGaps: Most Plum Village lay sanghas are informal Inter-Sangha groups not individually directoried
Some AZI affiliated dojos (Conthey/Valais) may have changed location
Kanzeon Sangha appears not to have a Swiss outpost (per kanzeon.de affiliate map)
Yun Hwa Dharma Sah (Bern/Zürich/Schwarzenburg/Luzern) excluded as the SBU classifies them Mahāyāna and the lineage is not Zen-canonical
Bodhidharma Chan-Gemeinschaft (Linji) noted in Switzerland but no current public address located

places.name: Bendōji
places.notes: Listed as the official Sōtōshū-registered temple in Switzerland.

places.name: Muijōji – Zen Dōjō Zürich
places.notes: Founded 1975 by Taisen Deshimaru; 'Muijō' = Fortress of Non-Fear. Also listed by Sōtōshū outside-Japan directory.

places.name: Mushin Zen Dōjō Zürich
places.notes: Affiliated with Association Zen Internationale; Dōgen-Deshimaru transmission line.

places.name: Dōjō Zen de Lausanne
places.notes: Member of AZI since founding in 1980; also SBU member.

places.name: Dōjō Zen Sansui
places.notes: Opened 1992 in Vevey, moved to Clarens; directed by Chantal Baiettini (ordained by Roland Yuno Rech).

places.name: Dōjō Zen de Genève (AZI / Groupe de Zazen)
places.notes: AZI affiliate; SBU member.

places.name: Prieuré Sōtō Zen du Sermon sur la Montagne
places.notes: Independent Sōtō priory.

places.name: Ryūkai Dōjō Zen (Kosen Sangha Genève)
places.notes: Kosen Sangha (Stéphane Kosen Thibaut) lineage; rooted in Deshimaru transmission.

places.name: Temple Zen Suimei Shōja
places.notes: Operated under the Kōsetsu-ji monastic federation.

places.name: Kōbokudō – Centre Zen de La Chaux-de-Fonds
places.notes: Urban centre attached to Kōsetsu-ji monastery; SBU member.

places.name: Kōsetsu-ji – Monastère Bouddhiste Sōtō Zen
places.notes: Residential Sōtō monastery in the Jura; flagship of zen-soto.ch network.

places.name: Kōsetsu-ji – Zazen-Gruppe Freiburg
places.notes: Satellite zazen group of Kōsetsu-ji monastery; SBU member.

places.name: Groupe de Zazen d'Yverdon
places.notes: Affiliated to the zen-soto.ch / Kōsetsu-ji federation.

places.name: Zen Dōjō Mokushinzan
places.notes: Basel zazen group; SBU member.

places.name: Groupe Zen de Conthey (Dōjō Zen Valais)
places.notes: Valais regional dōjō.

places.name: Dōjō Zen de Savagnier
places.notes: One of the rare Rinzai-tradition dōjōs in Switzerland; listed in bouddha.ch national directory.

places.name: Stiftung Felsentor / Hōshinji
places.notes: Founded by Vanja Palmers in lineage of Kobun Chino Otogawa Roshi; mountainside retreat centre.

places.name: Zendo am Fluss
places.lineage: Sōtō / Kobun Chino lineage (Kobun–David Steindl-Rast–Vanja Palmers)
places.notes: Urban sister-zendo of Felsentor; founded with Vanja Palmers.

places.name: Zen-Zentrum im Grünen Ring
places.notes: Identifies with Zen Peacemakers (Bernie Glassman / White Plum descent).

places.name: Kwan Um Zen Schweiz – Head Temple Zürich
places.notes: Listed as Kwan Um Switzerland head temple; guiding teacher historically Zen Master Wu Bong.

places.name: Plum Village Swiss Inter-Sangha
places.lineage: Plum Village / Vietnamese Thiền (Thich Nhat Hanh)
places.notes: Umbrella for Plum Village lay sanghas across Switzerland; coordinates national days of mindfulness.
